meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
family.definition: This major group comprises middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 0. The occupations classified under TEER 0, as those included in this group with zero (0) as the second digit of their code, are considered management occupations.
family.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

units.title: Corporate sales managers
units.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
units.sub_major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
units.definition: Corporate sales managers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the activities of establishments and departments involved in commercial, industrial, institutional, e-business and wholesale and retail sales. They are employed by commercial, industrial and wholesale and retail trade establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Corporate sales managers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the activities of establishments and departments involved in commercial, industrial, institutional, e-business and wholesale and retail sales.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in business or in a related field with a specialization in sales andseveral years of experience as a sales representative or in a related occupation are usually required.
additional_information: Progression to senior management positions is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: sales district manager (except insurance and real estate)

core_competencies.statement: to monitor and evaluate the spending and sales activities of departments or establishments
to negotiate with volume purchasers to enhance sales conditions and to negotiate favourable contracts with advertising agencies
to ensure instructions, strategies and presentations are easily understood when directing sales activities or negotiating contracts
to read important information to make informed decisions, plan sales strategies and communicate effectively with their team, clients and stakeholders
to direct and motivate the staff of sales departments in commercial, industrial, wholesale and other non-retail establishments
to understand sales reports and market analyses to determine customer needs, potential volume and competitive stretegies

units.title: Retail and wholesale trade managers
units.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
units.sub_major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
units.definition: Retail and wholesale trade managers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations of establishments that sell merchandise or services on a retail or wholesale basis. They are employed by retail and wholesale sales establishments or they may own and operate their own store.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Retail and wholesale trade managers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations of establishments that sell merchandise or services on a retail or wholesale basis.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A university degree or college diploma in business administration or other field related to the product or service being sold may be required.
Several years of related retail or wholesale sales experience at increasing levels of responsibility are usually required.
additional_information: There is some mobility between managers in this unit group, depending on the product or service.

example_titles.job_title_text: floor coverings and draperies store manager
leather goods and clothing store manager
television and stereo equipment store manager

core_competencies.statement: to ensure products are available to meet customer demand while avoiding overstocking or stock shortages
to negotiate favourable purchase contracts with sellers and buy goods at the best possible price with the aim of maximizing their resale price
to clearly explain products or services, answer customer inquiries and resolve complaints in a polite and professional manner
to study market research and trends to determine consumer demand, potential sales volumes and to plan budgets
to develop and instruct employees on market and sales strategies to improve sales and customer service
to understand budgeting, cost control, pricing strategies and profit margins to ensure the financial health of businesses

units.title: Restaurant and food service managers
units.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
units.sub_major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
units.minor_group_title: Managers in food service and accommodation
units.definition: Restaurant and food service managers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations of restaurants, bars, cafeterias and other food and beverage services. They are employed in food and beverage service establishments, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Restaurant and food service managers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations of restaurants, bars, cafeterias and other food and beverage services.
employment_requirements: Completion of a college or other program related to hospitality or food and beverage service management is usually required.
Responsible beverage service certification is usually required for managers of establishments serving alcoholic beverages.
Several years of experience in the food service sector, including supervisory experience, are required.
additional_information: Progression to senior management in food service is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: hotel food and beverage service manager

core_competencies.statement: to create and monitor budgets for various operational areas, including staffing, food inventory and marketing
to oversee food and supplies stock is maintained to meet demand of clients or upcoming events, research and order the best priced items without over overstocking
to clearly communicate tasks to others to reduce the chances of errors in food preparation or service
to identify problems with food preparation, customer service or cleanliness early to maintain high standards and ensure customer satisfaction
to systematically track sales trends, customer preferences and financial performance
to recruit and train staff on food and customer service standards and ensure standards are maintained
to understand business principles in budgeting, financial planning and cost control to maintain profitability and financial stability

units.title: Accommodation service managers
units.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
units.sub_major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
units.minor_group_title: Managers in food service and accommodation
units.definition: Accommodation service managers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations of an accommodation establishment or of a department within such an establishment. They are employed by hotels, motels, resorts, student residences and other accommodation establishments, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Accommodation service managers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations of an accommodation establishment or of a department within such an establishment.
employment_requirements: Several years of experience within the accommodation industry are usually required and may substitute for formal educational requirements.
A university degree or college diploma in hotel management or other related discipline is usually required for managers employed by hotel chains or large accommodation establishments.

core_competencies.statement: to ensure supplies, such as cleaning, linen and amenities supplies, are well stocked in order to maintain smooth and efficient operations
to gauge the needs and preferences of guests to tailor services and address issues proactively
to read and implement complex company policies, procedures and regulations
to resolve customer complaints, participate in the development of pricing and promotional strategies and to recruit staff
to motivate and guide staff, fostering a positive and welcoming work environment
to understand business management principles to organize and oversee daily operations efficiently, such as managing staff and scheduling, ensuring all departments work cohesively

units.title: Managers in customer and personal services
units.major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
units.sub_major_group_title: Middle management occupations in retail and wholesale trade and customer services
units.minor_group_title: Managers in customer and personal services
units.definition: Managers in customer and personal services provide services such as dry cleaning, hairdressing or residential cleaning. This group also includes managers of schools that provide non-vocational instruction in driving, languages, music, dance, art, cooking or fashion.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Managers in customer and personal services
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Managers in customer and personal services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Managers in customer and personal services include managers of establishments that provide services such as dry cleaning, hairdressing or residential cleaning. This unit group also includes managers of schools that provide non-vocational instruction in driving, languages, music, dance, art, cooking or fashion.
employment_requirements: A college diploma or vocational certificate in the subject of instruction may be required.
One to three years of experience in the service being offered are usually required.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.

core_competencies.statement: to control costs by ensuring materials are used efficiently and waste is limited
to monitor employee performance to provide constructive feedback, recognize achievements and identify areas for development
to adapt management strategies and processes to the unique needs of each services
to identify the cause of operational inefficiencies or customer complaints by drawing logical conclusions based on available evidence
to ensure customer interactions, operational processes and marketing work seamlessly together to deliver a cohesive customer experience
to understand business management principles to develop and implement goals, plan budgets and identify opportunities for expansion or improvement for personal or customer services
